_comment: Source of truth for Labs/Demos cards. index.html renders cards from this into the empty .projects-grid containers. featured = full-width + ordered first within section; visible = regular cards. Optional `topics` (array of strings) is rendered as pills under the description — only set this on cards whose source repo is private or org-owned, since public jonasneves/* repos pull topics from GitHub via enhanceLabsAndDemos(). Optional `publicRepo: true` opts a card into a static GitHub-icon link to https://github.com/<repo>; set it on org-owned cards whose repo is publicly viewable but not in the user's fetched list. Skip it for private-source cards (the link would 404 for visitors; owners still get the icon dynamically via the enhancer). Optional `webstore` is a Chrome Web Store listing URL: set it on published extensions to opt the card into a store-icon shortcut (left of the GitHub icon). The icon renders only when `href` doesn't already equal `webstore`, so a card linking straight to the store gets no redundant icon — set it on all published extensions anyway to record the listing; the gate decides whether it shows. hide_from_repos filters the dynamic Repos browse list (which is owner-only and also drops archived repos). Optional `icon` is a sprite-symbol id (without the `icon-` prefix) defined in index.html — controls the small stroked glyph before the project name. Sibling convention with me/docs/index.html's .card-icon. Description copy is written for a cold reader: what it does → for whom → one credibility hook; visible-card descs stay ≤ ~100 chars so the 2-line clamp never cuts mid-sentence.

featured.name: OPAL — Orchestrated Parallel Agent Loops
featured.desc: Drive full agentic Claude Code from any browser — direct over WebRTC, no relay, no tunnel. GitHub-gated; runs on your machine, on your Claude subscription.

featured.name: Better Robotics
featured.desc: Browser-native robotics education — pair an ESP32 or Raspberry Pi over Bluetooth and drive it with JavaScript. Duke Pratt research led by Prof. Jon Reifschneider.

visible.name: ros-mcp
visible.desc: Drive ROS 2 robots from an AI agent — topics, services, and actions as MCP tools.

visible.name: CatWatcher
visible.desc: Vision agent that identifies cats by name — in-browser VLM plus Claude.

visible.name: Student Benefits Hub
visible.desc: Student-tools directory curated end-to-end by an AI agent.

visible.name: Cuko
visible.desc: Turns any PDF or lecture into predict-then-reveal exercises.

visible.name: CanvasFlow
visible.desc: AI assistance and schedule planning inside Canvas LMS — on the Chrome Web Store.

visible.name: TapTo
visible.desc: Reprogrammable NFC & QR tags — repoint anytime without rewriting. On the Chrome Web Store.

visible.name: TallyHo
visible.desc: Point a phone at a shelf and it keeps inventory — VLM + Claude.
